images.caption: Figure 1:Exposing calibrated confidence and grounding signals to the agent each turn yields better action choices.Left: without telemetry, the agent retrieves additional evidence but still stops with an unsupported answer for the subquestion, causing an incorrect final answer.Right:CalVerTexposes low confidence and weak grounding in the form of verifier telemetry, so the agent first retrieves when the current evidence is insufficient, then refines the answer for targeted retrieval, and only commits after the answer is well grounded.

header: Appendix ACalibration scores of the telemetry signals

images.caption: Figure 2:Reliability diagrams for the two telemetry signals.Top:empirical accuracy per confidence bin, with the dashed line marking perfect calibration.Bottom:per-bin sample countsNN.Left(MiniCheck on AggreFact-RAG): the well populated high-confidence region exists at or above the diagonal, indicating MiniCheck to be confident for correctly grounded answers.Right(DiNCoon TriviaQA) bars closely track the diagonal across the well-populated region, yielding strong calibration.

header: Appendix CFLOP analysis of prompt only setups

images.caption: Figure 3:Effect of telemetry across question difficulty and entity frequency.Deltas are computed against+teland-telfor Qwen3-32B.(a)On HotpotQA, the F1 gain increases with hop count, suggesting that telemetry helps most on harder multi-hop questions.(b)WiTQA gain and retrieval rate increase concentrate on the rarest subject popularity buckets and diminish on popular entities.

header: Appendix GFull Example: agent actions flip upon exposed telemetry
